homedir.h(3) Library Functions Manual homedir.h(3)

NAME

homedir.h - Temporary GPG homedir management for isolated key operations.

SYNOPSIS

Typedefs


typedef struct gpg_homedir gpg_homedir_t

Functions


gpg_homedir_t * gpg_homedir_create (void)
Create a temporary GPG homedir for isolated key operations. const char * gpg_homedir_path (const gpg_homedir_t *homedir)
Get the homedir path for use in GPG commands. void gpg_homedir_destroy (gpg_homedir_t *homedir)
Destroy a temporary GPG homedir and clean up all files.

Detailed Description

Temporary GPG homedir management for isolated key operations.

Provides utilities for creating and managing temporary GPG homedirs to isolate key operations (import, decrypt, sign) without polluting the user's main GPG keyring.

Using a temporary homedir provides:

  • Isolation from user's keys (no risk of deleting wrong keys)
  • Automatic cleanup (just delete the directory)
  • Better error handling and race condition avoidance
  • Cleaner, more maintainable code

Example usage:

gpg_homedir_t homedir = gpg_homedir_create();
if (homedir == NULL) {


  return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Failed to create GPG homedir");
}
// Use homedir.path in gpg commands
char cmd[1024];
snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "gpg --homedir '%s' --batch --import '%s'",


         homedir->path, key_file);
int status = system(cmd);
gpg_homedir_destroy(homedir);

Definition in file homedir.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct gpg_homedir gpg_homedir_t

Definition at line 46 of file homedir.h.

Function Documentation

gpg_homedir_t * gpg_homedir_create (void )

Create a temporary GPG homedir for isolated key operations. Creates a new temporary directory configured for GPG use. The directory is isolated from the user's main GPG keyring.

Returns

Pointer to homedir handle on success, NULL on failure Caller must destroy with gpg_homedir_destroy()

Note

The directory is created in the system temp location (TMPDIR, /tmp, etc.)

Definition at line 22 of file homedir.c.

22                                         {
23   gpg_homedir_t *homedir = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(gpg_homedir_t), gpg_homedir_t *);
24   if (!homedir) {
25     log_error("Failed to allocate memory for GPG homedir handle");
26     return NULL;
27   }
28 
29   /* Create temporary directory using platform abstraction */
30   if (platform_mkdtemp(homedir->path, sizeof(homedir->path), "ascii-chat-gpg") != 0) {
31     log_error("Failed to create temporary GPG homedir");
32     SAFE_FREE(homedir);
33     return NULL;
34   }
35 
36   /* Restrict permissions to owner only (mode 0700) using platform abstraction */
37   if (platform_chmod(homedir->path, 0700) != 0) {
38     log_warn("Failed to set permissions on GPG homedir, attempting cleanup");
39     platform_rmdir_recursive(homedir->path);
40     SAFE_FREE(homedir);
41     return NULL;
42   }
43 
44   log_debug("Created temporary GPG homedir: %s", homedir->path);
45   return homedir;
46 }




References log_debug, log_error, log_warn,
    platform_chmod(), platform_mkdtemp(),
    platform_rmdir_recursive(), SAFE_FREE, and
  SAFE_MALLOC.






void
  gpg_homedir_destroy (gpg_homedir_t * homedir)


Destroy a temporary GPG homedir and clean up all files.
    Recursively deletes the temporary directory and all its contents. Safe to
    call on NULL pointer (no-op).




Parameters


homedir Handle from gpg_homedir_create()
  (can be NULL)


Definition at line 55 of file homedir.c.


55                                                  {
56   if (!homedir) {
57     return;
58   }
59 
60   /* Recursively delete the entire directory and all contents using platform abstraction */
61   if (platform_rmdir_recursive(homedir->path) != 0) {
62     log_warn("Failed to completely clean up GPG homedir: %s", homedir->path);
63   } else {
64     log_debug("Cleaned up temporary GPG homedir: %s", homedir->path);
65   }
66 
67   SAFE_FREE(homedir);
68 }






References log_debug, log_warn,
    platform_rmdir_recursive(), and SAFE_FREE.






const
  char * gpg_homedir_path (const gpg_homedir_t * homedir)


Get the homedir path for use in GPG commands. Returns the
    filesystem path of the temporary homedir. This path should be used with
    gpg's --homedir flag.




Parameters


homedir Handle from
  gpg_homedir_create()


Returns


Pointer to path string (valid until
  gpg_homedir_destroy() is called) Returns NULL if homedir is
  invalid


Definition at line 48 of file homedir.c.


48                                                            {
49   if (!homedir) {
50     return NULL;
51   }
52   return homedir->path;
53 }
